title: Capsense Based P300 Protocol for BCI with Finger Evoked Potentials
author: Dr Bheemaiah Anil K Kumar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Abstract: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) have the potential to revolutionize the way humans interact with technology. One promising approach for BCIs is to use finger evoked potentials detected through Capsense technology. In this paper, we present a P300 protocol for analyzing finger evoked potentials using Capsense technology. Our protocol uses a cloud function to capture data from a Capsense channel, segment the data using a template-based approach, and generate a spike train. We then apply a P300 algorithm to the spike train to detect the P300 response. Our protocol provides a reliable and accurate method for detecting P300 responses in finger evoked potentials and can be used for a wide range of BCI applications.
